description: Unified session list: show all non-cron sessions across channels, with session_name from first user message, cross-channel conversation continuation, session header with copy buttons, and filtered conversation history

userStories.title: Add listSessionsFiltered method to filter out cron-only sessions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend method to list sessions excluding those that only have cron tasks, so the UI only shows user-initiated conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `listSessionsFiltered(options: { excludeCronOnly?: boolean })` method to SessionStore
A session is 'cron-only' if ALL its tasks have source_type = 'cron' (i.e. no web/feishu user messages)
Use SQL subquery: exclude sessions where NOT EXISTS a task with source_type != 'cron'
Method still returns sessions ordered by updated_at DESC, with configurable limit (default 100)
Existing `listSessions()` method remains unchanged for backward compatibility
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Key SQL pattern: WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tasks t WHERE t.session_id = s.id AND t.source_type != 'cron' AND t.source_type IS NOT NULL) — sessions with zero tasks should still appear (new empty sessions)

userStories.title: Update WorkspaceService.getSnapshot to use filtered session list
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the workspace snapshot to use the filtered session list so the web UI automatically shows only non-cron sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `WorkspaceService.getSnapshot()` to call `listSessionsFiltered({ excludeCronOnly: true })` instead of `listSessions()`
The snapshot still includes all session fields (id, name, title, agentType, channelType, status, updatedAt)
selectedSessionId fallback logic (pick first from filtered list) still works
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Ensure session_name is always set from first user message
userStories.description: As a user, I want every session to display my first message as the session name, regardless of channel (web or feishu).
acceptanceCriteria: Verify `persistInitialSessionName` is called in both web and feishu inbound paths
Verify `summarizeSessionName` truncates to ~50 chars and strips markdown/slashes
Sessions created via web message flow call persistInitialSessionName after first user message insert
Sessions created via feishu message flow call persistInitialSessionName after first user message insert
Session name is empty only when no user message has been sent yet
WorkspaceSessionSummary.name field uses first user message content when session.name is empty
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The `getSnapshot` already calls `getFirstUserMessages` and `getSessionName`. Verify these are wired correctly for all channel types.

userStories.title: Add session header bar with name, ID, and copy buttons to chat panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the session name and session ID at the top of the chat panel, each with a copy button, so I can easily reference and share session identifiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new `chat-header` div at the top of the workspace chat-main section in AppShell
Header displays: session name (bold, truncated with ellipsis if too long) + session ID (monospace, truncated)
Each element has a copy-to-clipboard button with ClipboardCopy icon
Copy button shows brief visual feedback (e.g. icon change or tooltip) on click
Header is hidden when no session is selected (messages.length === 0 and no sessionId)
Header does not scroll with messages (position: sticky top)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Session name comes from props.sessions.find(s => s.id === props.sessionId)?.name. Session ID is props.sessionId. Add CSS class `chat-header` with styling matching the existing app theme.

userStories.title: Enable cross-channel conversation continuation from session list
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click any session in the list (web or feishu origin) and continue the conversation from the web UI, so I can seamlessly resume any session regardless of its original channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting any session in the sidebar loads its messages in the chat panel
The send message API (`POST /api/sessions/:sessionId/messages`) already works for any session ID regardless of channel — verify this path accepts messages for feishu-originated sessions
When sending a message to a feishu-originated session from web, the message is stored with sourceType 'web' and the response is delivered back via web SSE (not feishu)
The chat input bar (textarea + send button) is always visible when a session is selected
Sending a message creates a task and starts a run on the selected session
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The inbound service `receiveOnSession` already handles messages on arbitrary sessions. The web message route handler needs to support specifying any sessionId. Check if the web channel currently restricts to web-only sessions.

userStories.title: Filter meaningless agent messages from conversation display
userStories.description: As a user, I want the chat panel to hide empty or meaningless agent replies (pure thinking blocks, empty responses) so the conversation reads cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add filtering logic in the message rendering loop of AppShell workspace section
Filter out messages where role='assistant' AND (content is empty string OR content is only whitespace OR content matches thinking-only pattern like '<thinking>...</thinking>' with no other content)
Filter out messages where role='system' and content starts with 'Run succeeded' (already partially done) OR content is empty
User messages are never filtered
Normal agent responses with actual content are never filtered
Filtering is client-side only — raw messages remain stored in the database unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The current rendering already has `if (message.role === 'system') return null;` and special handling for 'Run succeeded'. Extend this to also filter empty/thinking-only assistant messages. Consider a helper function `isMessageDisplayable(message)`.

userStories.title: Load full conversation history for selected session
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the complete conversation history when I select a session, not just the latest 200 messages, so I can review the full context.
acceptanceCriteria: Increase the message limit in `getLatestBySession` call within `getSnapshot` from 200 to a higher limit or remove the limit entirely for conversation display
Alternatively, add a pagination mechanism: load first 200, then lazy-load older messages on scroll-to-top
For MVP: increasing the limit to 1000 is acceptable
Messages are ordered by created_at ASC (oldest first) — verify this ordering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code: `this.messages.getLatestBySession(sessionId, 200)`. The MessageStore.getLatestBySession already accepts a limit parameter. For MVP, just increase to 1000. For later, add cursor-based pagination.
